title: amyema miraculosa subsp. miraculosa
intro: Amyema miraculosa subsp. miraculosa, also known as the Western Australian dodonaea mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that grows on the branches of host trees, particularly the Dodonaea viscosa. The plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Amyema miraculosa subsp. miraculosa include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused in hot water, and its extracts are also used in creams and ointments to treat various skin ailments.
